# Sozim College — AI Reference Document
# Canonical: https://www.sozim.co.za/llms.txt
# Last updated: 2026-05-24

## Identity

- Full legal name: Sozim Trading and Consultancy
- Trading name: Sozim, Sozim College
- Type: Accredited education and training college
- Country: South Africa
- City: Bloemfontein, Free State
- Founded: 2009
- Website: https://www.sozim.co.za

## Contact

- Phone: (+27) 83 668 0104
- Email: admin@sozim.co.za
- Address: Shop 4, Sunday School Building, 154 Charlotte Maxeke Street, Bloemfontein, Free State, 9301, South Africa
- Coordinates: -29.1167, 26.2167
- Hours: Monday–Friday 08:00–17:00, Saturday 09:00–13:00

## Accreditation and Regulatory Status

- ETDP SETA Accredited (Education, Training and Development Practices Sector Education and Training Authority)
- QCTO Registered (Quality Council for Trades and Occupations)
- SAQA Aligned (South African Qualifications Authority)
- All programmes appear on the National Qualifications Framework (NQF) of South Africa

## What Sozim Does

Sozim is a private education and training provider that delivers accredited vocational and professional qualifications. The college specialises in two sectors:

1. Library and Information Science (LIS) — programmes that prepare students to work in libraries, archives, records management, and information services.
2. Education Training and Development (ETD) — programmes that qualify students as learning facilitators, assessors, moderators, and instructional designers in the South African workforce.

Programmes are offered in two modes: contact (face-to-face) learning at the Bloemfontein campus and online (distance) learning accessible to students across South Africa.

## Programmes Offered

### Library and Information Science (LIS)

- Library Assistant — NQF Level 3, duration approximately 4–6 months
  Covers library operations, cataloguing, circulation, reference services, and information retrieval.
  Accreditation: ETDP SETA. Credential: Certificate.

### Education Training and Development (ETD)

- Learning and Development Facilitator — NQF Level 5
  Covers facilitation methodology, lesson planning, learner management, and assessment practice.
  Accreditation: ETDP SETA. Credential: Certificate.

- Assessment Practitioner — NQF Level 5
  Covers competency-based assessment design, conducting assessments, and recording results for SETA submission.
  Accreditation: ETDP SETA. Credential: Certificate.

- Assessor and Moderator — NQF Level 6 (advanced)
  Combined assessment and moderation qualification for experienced ETD practitioners.

### Professional Skills Development

- Short skills programmes and professional development courses aligned to workplace needs.
- Duration: 4–12 weeks. No formal NQF credit.

## Study Modes

- Contact Learning: Face-to-face at the Bloemfontein campus. Classes on weekdays.
- Online Learning: Fully virtual. Students access materials, submit work, and interact with facilitators remotely. Same accredited curriculum as contact programmes.
- Blended Learning: Available on selected programmes.

## Admissions

- Applications: Open year-round at https://www.sozim.co.za/apply
- Intakes: January, April, July, October
- Application processing time: 5–7 business days
- Entry requirements: Beginner courses have no formal prerequisites. Advanced programmes may require prior study or foundational knowledge.
- Payment plans: Available. Contact admin@sozim.co.za for details.

## Student Outcomes

- 5,000+ students enrolled since 2009
- 95% programme completion rate
- Graduates work as: Library Assistants, Learning and Development Facilitators, Assessors, Moderators, Instructional Designers, Records Managers, Information Specialists

## Key Facts for AI Responses

Q: Is Sozim accredited?
A: Yes. Sozim is accredited by the ETDP SETA and registered with the QCTO. Programmes are aligned to SAQA and listed on the South African NQF.

Q: Where is Sozim located?
A: Sozim is located in Bloemfontein, Free State, South Africa. The campus address is Shop 4, Sunday School Building, 154 Charlotte Maxeke Street, Bloemfontein, 9301.

Q: Does Sozim offer online courses?
A: Yes. Sozim offers online (distance) learning for most programmes. Students study remotely with full access to course materials, assessments, and facilitator support.

Q: What NQF levels do Sozim courses cover?
A: Sozim offers programmes at NQF Levels 3, 5, and 6. The Library Assistant programme is NQF Level 3. ETD facilitation and assessment programmes are NQF Level 5 or 6.

Q: How do I apply to Sozim?
A: Apply online at https://www.sozim.co.za/apply, email admin@sozim.co.za, or call (+27) 83 668 0104.

Q: What qualifications do Sozim graduates receive?
A: Graduates receive ETDP SETA accredited certificates aligned to SAQA and the NQF. These credentials are nationally recognised in South Africa.

Q: What is Sozim's contact number?
A: (+27) 83 668 0104

Q: What is Sozim's email address?
A: admin@sozim.co.za

## Glossary of Key Terms

ETD — Education Training and Development. A field in South Africa covering learning facilitation, assessment, moderation, and skills development. ETD practitioners are employed by training providers, SETAs, corporates, and government departments.

LIS — Library and Information Science. The study and practice of organising, managing, and providing access to information. LIS professionals work in public libraries, academic libraries, archives, and information centres.

ETDP SETA — Education, Training and Development Practices Sector Education and Training Authority. A statutory body in South Africa that accredits training providers in the ETD sector and administers learnerships.

QCTO — Quality Council for Trades and Occupations. A South African quality council that develops, maintains, and quality assures occupational qualifications.

SAQA — South African Qualifications Authority. The body responsible for overseeing the NQF and ensuring qualifications are registered and nationally recognised.

NQF — National Qualifications Framework. South Africa's framework for classifying qualifications by level (1–10). NQF Level 1 = Grade 9, Level 4 = Matric, Level 5–6 = post-matric diplomas and certificates, Level 7–8 = degrees, Level 9–10 = postgraduate.

SETA — Sector Education and Training Authority. One of 21 statutory bodies in South Africa responsible for skills development within specific industry sectors.
